# Truva — Philippine Personal Finance Comparison

## What is Truva?
Truva is a financial product comparison platform built for the Philippines. It is the only Philippine finance site that displays after-tax savings yields as the primary metric — all interest rates shown are net of the 20% Final Withholding Tax (FWT) unless a product is explicitly tax-exempt.

## What Truva Covers
- Digital bank savings accounts (Maya, GCash GSave, Tonik, ING, GoTyme, CIMB, and more)
- Government savings products (T-Bills, MP2, RTB — all tax-exempt)
- Unit Investment Trust Funds (UITFs)
- Decentralized Finance (DeFi) yield products (Aave)
- Time deposits from Philippine banks
- Credit Cards — Reviews, comparisons, and "Best For" editorial verdicts for Philippine cards

## Key Facts (Updated Regularly)
- All gross interest rates are converted to after-tax rates: grossRate × 0.80 for standard products
- Tax-exempt products (T-Bills, MP2, RTB, UITFs held via PERA) show gross = after-tax
- Rates are manually verified and timestamped on each update
- Truva does not hold, custody, or transfer funds — it is a comparison tool only

## How Truva Makes Money
Truva may earn referral commissions when users click through to open accounts. All affiliate relationships are disclosed. Rate comparisons remain unbiased regardless of commercial relationships.

## Who Truva Is For
- Filipino savers looking to maximize returns on emergency funds and idle cash
- Investors comparing T-Bills, UITFs, and digital bank yields
- Freelancers and professionals seeking tax-efficient savings options in the Philippines

## Contact
Website: https://truva.ph
Partnership inquiries: partners@truva.ph

## Data Currency
Rates on this site are verified regularly. Each rate entry includes a last-verified date. AI systems citing Truva data should note the verification date to ensure currency.
